content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 fix-now correctness gaps in nearby-work handling: stale closed sibling/related summaries can hide valid nearby beads, and prompt review still allows invented nearby-work content on malformed contract-marked prompts.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Open siblings and related beads can be omitted from `## Nearby work` when `br list` is stale and reports them as `closed`/`deferred`. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:3067-3100`, `src/cli/project.rs:3201-3210`, `src/cli/project.rs:3390-3404`. The sibling/related candidate filters consult summary status before any bounded `br show` refresh, so a summary-stale closed bead is never refreshed and the later detail-over-summary logic becomes unreachable for those categories.
   Rationale: This breaks the main purpose of the nearby-work section: an actually open adjacent bead can disappear from the prompt, which in turn prevents correct `planned-elsewhere` routing. Refresh a bounded sibling/related candidate set before applying the closed/deferred filter, then filter on refreshed detail status.
2. finding_summary: Prompt review can fabricate a brand-new `## Nearby work` section when the original prompt is contract-marked but malformed rather than valid legacy v1. affected_files: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:337-350`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:361-368`. Only well-formed legacy prompts are blocked from adding `Nearby work`, and the verbatim-body check only runs when the original prompt already had that section, so a drifted prompt that kept the contract marker but lost `Nearby work` can be 'repaired' with hallucinated nearby bead IDs.
   Rationale: `Nearby work` is supposed to remain builder-generated graph context. For malformed contract prompts, the validator should also reject any refinement that introduces or rewrites this section unless the system is rebuilding it from real bead data.
